sequence_data.subject_line: Quick question for CTOs scaling engineering

output.triage: ⚠️ CRITICAL — this sequence needs a complete rebuild before sending another email. Deliverability is severely impaired (86%), open rate is catastrophic (14%), reply rate is 1.0%, and unsubscribe rate at 4.0% is damaging your sender reputation. Do not send any more touches from this sequence until deliverability is diagnosed and copy is rewritten.

benchmarks.deliverability: POOR — below 90% threshold. Likely spam folder placement.
benchmarks.open_rate: POOR — below 15% indicates deliverability failure or severe subject line problem.
benchmarks.reply_rate: POOR — 1.0% indicates wrong ICP, wrong angle, or copy quality issue.
benchmarks.unsubscribe: CRITICAL — 4.0% unsubscribe rate is 4-8x normal. Damaging sender domain reputation daily.

touch_analysis.assessment: CRITICAL: 28 of 200 emails never arrived (likely spam). 14% open rate on delivered emails suggests subject line failure AND deliverability issues. Subject line 'Quick question for CTOs scaling engineering' is one of the most overused cold email subject lines in 2024-2025 — spam filters flag it. The 4.6% unsubscribe rate on Touch 1 means the audience and message are severely misaligned.

CRITICAL: 'Following up' subject line is among the lowest-performing cold email subjects. Declining opens confirm the audience has checked out. High unsubscribe rate continuing to damage sender reputation.
STOP — Touch 3 has 0 positive replies and a 6.3% unsubscribe rate. Every send is actively damaging the sender domain. This touch should be deleted from the sequence immediately.

attribution_findings: Generic subject lines ('Quick question', 'Following up', 'One more thought') are producing open rates 3-4x below benchmark for this audience — these are among the most spam-filtered subject lines in B2B email.
28 emails never delivered from send #1 (86% deliverability) — likely SPF/DKIM misconfiguration or domain reputation issue from prior high-volume sends.

red_flags.action: IMMEDIATE: Check SPF, DKIM, and DMARC records. Check Google Postmaster Tools for domain reputation score. Stop all sends from this domain until deliverability issue is resolved.

Complete rewrite of subject lines required. All 3 subject lines used ('Quick question', 'Following up', 'One more thought') are in the bottom 10% of performers. Rebuild with specific hooks (funding, hiring, tech stack, role milestone).
PAUSE SEQUENCE NOW. 4x the acceptable unsubscribe rate is actively damaging sender reputation with every email sent. This will cascade into worse deliverability and accelerate the failure spiral.
Complete copy rebuild required. Fix deliverability first, then rewrite all 3 emails from scratch with prospect-specific hooks and a value proposition that addresses CTO-specific pain.

recommendations.finding: 86% deliverability indicates DNS or domain reputation problem
recommendations.action: Run SPF/DKIM/DMARC check immediately. Check Google Postmaster Tools domain reputation. Consider warming a new sending subdomain (outreach.yourdomain.com) if main domain reputation is damaged. Do not send any outreach until this is resolved.
recommendations.expected_lift: Fixing deliverability alone could double effective reach (86% → 97%+ deliverability)

recommendations.finding: All 3 subject lines are generic and spam-filtered
recommendations.action: Rewrite every subject line with specificity. Minimum: include company name or prospect name. Better: reference a specific trigger (recent funding, hiring post, news event). 'Saw [Company] raised Series B last month' vs. 'Quick question for CTOs'.
recommendations.expected_lift: Specific subject lines typically achieve 30-45% open rate for this ICP vs. current 14%

recommendations.finding: Copy doesn't reference anything specific to the prospect
recommendations.action: Rebuild all 3 email bodies using prospect-specific hooks from company research. Each email should reference a specific, verifiable fact about the company or the prospect's recent activity. Generic copy is the primary driver of the low reply rate.
recommendations.expected_lift: Personalized hooks typically produce 3-5x the reply rate of generic templates for technical audiences

recommendations.finding: Touch 3 actively destroying sender reputation with 6.3% unsubscribe
recommendations.action: Delete Touch 3 from this sequence entirely. Replace with a breakup email: 'Last one from me — if onboarding automation isn't a priority right now, totally understand. Worth connecting when it is.' Short, low-friction, high-reply.
recommendations.expected_lift: Breakup emails typically generate 30-40% of sequence replies and have 0.2-0.4% unsubscribe vs current 6.3%
